skill.name: aws-secrets-rotation
skill.description: Automate rotation of secrets, credentials, and API keys using AWS Secrets Manager and Lambda
skill.summary: Implement automated secrets rotation for RDS, DocumentDB, Redshift, and third-party API keys with AWS-managed Lambda functions and compliance monitoring
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 70 static findings are false positives. The skill contains legitimate AWS CLI documentation, Lambda rotation code examples, and compliance tracking scripts. External command patterns are bash examples in markdown code blocks demonstrating AWS API usage. Network references are official AWS and Stripe API endpoints. No malicious patterns detected.

content.user_title: Automate AWS Secrets Rotation for RDS and API Keys
content.value_statement: Manual secrets rotation is error-prone and often neglected. This skill provides production-ready Lambda functions and AWS CLI commands to automate credential rotation for databases and third-party services.
actual_capabilities: Create and manage secrets in AWS Secrets Manager via CLI commands
Deploy Lambda functions for automatic RDS password rotation
Implement custom rotation logic for third-party API keys like Stripe
Configure CloudWatch alarms to monitor rotation failures
Generate compliance reports tracking rotation schedules
Execute emergency rotation for compromised credentials
limitations: Requires AWS credentials with Secrets Manager and Lambda permissions
Custom API key rotation requires API support for key regeneration
RDS rotation requires appropriate database user privileges
Does not support on-premises or non-AWS secret stores

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer Automating Database Credential Rotation
use_cases.description: Set up 30-day automatic rotation for production RDS MySQL credentials using AWS-managed Lambda templates with CloudWatch monitoring for rotation failures.

use_cases.title: Security Team Implementing Compliance Requirements
use_cases.description: Deploy rotation policies for all secrets, generate quarterly compliance reports showing rotation status, and configure alerts for overdue rotations.

use_cases.title: Developer Rotating Third-Party API Keys
use_cases.description: Create custom Lambda function to rotate Stripe API keys by calling their API, validating new keys, and revoking old credentials automatically.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Secret Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an AWS secret for my production MySQL database with username admin, host mydb.cluster-abc.us-east-1.rds.amazonaws.com, port 3306, and database myapp
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - Setting up your first secret

prompt_templates.title: Enable RDS Automatic Rotation
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up automatic rotation every 30 days for my RDS MySQL secret using the AWS-managed Lambda rotation function

prompt_templates.title: Custom API Key Rotation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Lambda function that rotates Stripe API keys by calling the Stripe API to generate new keys, test them, and revoke old ones

prompt_templates.title: Compliance Audit Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a compliance report listing all secrets without rotation enabled and those not rotated in over 90 days

output_examples.input: Set up rotation for production RDS credentials
output_examples.output: Rotation enabled for prod/db/mysql with 30-day schedule. Lambda function arn:aws:lambda:us-east-1:123456789012:function:SecretsManagerRDSMySQLRotation will automatically rotate credentials. First rotation initiated.

output_examples.input: Audit all secrets for rotation compliance
output_examples.output:
Compliant Secrets: 12
Non-Compliant Secrets: 3

Non-Compliant Details:
  - dev/test/api-key: Rotation not enabled
  - staging/db/postgres: Not rotated in 127 days
  - legacy/service-token: Never rotated

best_practices: Test rotation in non-production environments before deploying to production
Configure CloudWatch alarms to alert on rotation failures within 5 minutes
Maintain runbooks documenting emergency rotation procedures for compromised credentials
anti_patterns: Hardcoding secrets in application code instead of retrieving from Secrets Manager
Setting rotation intervals longer than 90 days for sensitive credentials
Rotating secrets without testing application compatibility first

faq.question: What AWS permissions are required to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need secretsmanager:CreateSecret, secretsmanager:GetSecretValue, secretsmanager:RotateSecret, secretsmanager:UpdateSecretVersionStage, lambda:InvokeFunction, and cloudwatch:PutMetricAlarm permissions.

faq.question: Can this rotate secrets for non-AWS databases?
faq.answer: Yes, you can create custom Lambda functions for any database or service that supports programmatic credential rotation via API.

faq.question: What happens if rotation fails?
faq.answer: The secret remains unchanged with the previous credentials. CloudWatch alarms can notify you of failures. The pending version is not promoted to current.

faq.question: How do I rotate a secret immediately after a breach?
faq.answer: Use the rotate-secret command with --rotate-immediately flag to force rotation regardless of the scheduled interval.

faq.question: Can applications access both old and new credentials during rotation?
faq.answer: Yes, AWS Secrets Manager supports multiple version stages (AWSPENDING, AWSCURRENT, AWSPREVIOUS) allowing gradual credential transitions.

faq.question: Is there a cost for using automatic rotation?
faq.answer: Yes, you pay for Lambda invocations (approximately monthly per secret) plus Secrets Manager API calls. Rotation Lambda runs cost about $0.20-0.50 per secret per month.
